name: Police Cadets & Explorers
description: The Police Cadet/Explorer program gives youth the opportunity to work directly with Stratford Police Officers to learn about leadership, self-confidence, and respect for others. The Junior Cadet program is offered to Stratford Middle School students. Registration is open year-round.
highlights: Junior Cadet program for middle schoolers

name: PAL Karate
description: PAL Karate is a year-round program held at the PAL Building on Tuesdays from 7–8 PM, open to ages 3 and over. All skill levels and abilities are welcome. Students also have the opportunity to attend and compete in local tournaments.
registration: Open year-round — contact us to join

name: PAL Hockey
description: With support from The Rinks at Shelton (Shelton Sports Center), PAL offers super low-cost Learn to Skate and Learn to Play Hockey programs each summer. Grant funding helps outfit up to 60 hockey players annually. Skating begins in July.
highlights: Learn to Skate & Learn to Play
registration: Registration opens in May each year

name: Baking Workshops
description: Baking workshops are held throughout the year and vary in length and difficulty, offering programs for all ages. If you have an idea or want to assist, reach out and we'll get another session started. Follow the website and social media for the next session.
registration: Check social media for upcoming sessions

name: Summer Camps
description: A staple of Stratford PAL, summer camps run every year out of the PAL Building and include fun-filled field trips. Registration opens in May for camps held in July. A beloved tradition for Stratford youth and families.
registration: Registration opens in May each year

name: COPfit
description: COPfit is a wellness, fitness, and exercise program developed by Stratford Officers for our youth. This 4-week program runs during the summer and fills quickly — keep an eye out for registration opening in May.
registration: Registration opens in May — spots fill fast

name: Fishing Club
description: The PAL Fishing Club is set up to bring anglers, first responders, and their families outdoors together. A new program launched in 2025, it creates a fun, community-building experience for the whole family.

name: Bike Squad
description: The Bike Squad was formed to give youth in the community a resource for learning bike safety, developing riding skills, and getting outdoors. Led by a dedicated team of officers from all skill levels at the Stratford Police Department.

name: Roots and Recipes
description: Youth and officers grow food in on-site gardens at the PAL Building, learning all aspects of gardening and healthy living. Over the course of the program, youth work with officers to create healthy recipes from what they grow — a true farm-to-table experience. A hit in 2025!
registration: Registration opens March — seedlings start soon after
